text:

Sport|Olympics

Little didTom Daleyknow that taking his family on a trip to the Olympic Museum in Colorado Springs would spark his Olympic U-turn.

Daley was greeted by a round of applause by staff there and, when a video played of what it meant to be an Olympian, he burst into tears. It was then his son Robbie said he wanted to watch him dive at theOlympics.

It was fitting that the six-year-old was in the stands at the Aquatics Centre in Paris today with younger brother Phoenix and Daley’s husband Dustin Lance Black to watch Daley complete his retirement U-turn and dive to silver with partner Noah Williams.

Such is the 30-year-old’s longevity in the sport, this was his fifth 10m synchro platform partner at his fifthOlympic Games, this medal adding to a gold from Tokyo in 2021 and three career bronze.

Williams, along with Jack Laugher, had caused a ripple of controversy in the build-up to the Games when it was revealed he had set up OnlyFans pages with risqué images in the hope of­ ­earning money on the side of their £28,000 allowance from the National Lottery.

Alas for British hopes, Chinese duo Junjie Lian and Hao Yang, the overwhelming pre-event favourites, caused the least ripple on the water of any of the eight pairs off the platform to deservedly take gold.

Keely Hodgkinson reveals Grand Slam Track decision and 2025 ambitions

Discover a hidden island paradise in the Indian Ocean

Daley and Williams had only been partnered together in November when Daley’s regular dive partner Matty Lee got injured.

Daley, like many of his British teammates, had been the inspiration for the Hackney-born Williams, then a gymnast, to take up the sport when given a leaflet for Crystal Palace Diving Club.

Despite training on different continents — Daley in the United States and Williams in the UK — they won silver a few months later at the World ­Championships.

In this competition, the British pair recorded exactly the same scores as the Canadian duo Rylan Wiens and Nathan Zsombor-Murray in the opening two rounds for a share of second behind China.

A third dive of 83.52 put them into an outright second before lining up for their second-hardest dive, a back three-and-a-half somersaults pike. It scored a huge 93.96, again only bettered by the Chinese.

Dive five saw them a little out of synch for the first time in the competition but they barely caused a splash and their score of 87.72 left them with a 28-point cushion with their hardest dive left, a forward four-and-a-half somersaults with a 3.7 degree of difficulty.

Greeted by a massive roar from the stands beforehand, they landed it with aplomb to score 93.24, leaving China with the penultimate dive of the competition to cement the gold. By that point, it was a mere formality.

Whether this is a last Games for Daley, who with Helen Glover was the flagbearer at the opening ceremony, is a moot point. That might rest in the hands of his two boys. It is worth nothing, there was a contestant 10 years his senior in the final in Gary Hunt, British but a Paris resident and having switched to Red Bull.

Hunt has been described by one competitor as “the Michael Jordan, Muhammad Ali, The Tiger Woods of cliff diving”, and used to play a Tarzan-like character at a French water park. He had won a Commonwealth Games bronze for England as long ago as 2006, a longevity to suggest the next Games — a sixth — in Los Angeles, where Daley has his home, might not be so far fetched.

title: Paris 2024: Tom Daley delivers super silver with Noah Williams after delaying Olympic farewell | The Standard
author: Matt Majendie
description: Unstoppable China soar to 10m synchro platform gold as Team GB duo come second
keywords: Tom Daley, Paris Olympics, Paris 2024, Olympic Games, Team GB
